meta.generator: SpeedyCAT BBLS AI-POC deterministic fact-bank generator
meta.disclaimer: AI-generated proof-of-concept questions. NOT real MCAT/AAMC content. Text-only (no HTML/SVG). Each item has exactly one correct answer and three distinct distractors; all items passed the 4-criterion automated eval.

questions.stem: In evolution, what is disruptive selection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: selection favoring both extremes over the intermediate phenotype

choices.label: B
choices.text: selection favoring intermediate phenotypes over the extremes

choices.label: C
choices.text: selection that maintains multiple alleles, as with sickle-cell heterozygotes

choices.label: D
choices.text: selection favoring one extreme phenotype, shifting the average

questions.explanation: disruptive selection is selection favoring both extremes over the intermediate phenotype.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: A canned food is heated to boiling (100 C) but some bacterial contaminants survive and later cause spoilage. Which bacterial feature best explains this survival?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Formation of heat-resistant endospores

choices.label: B
choices.text: Presence of a capsule

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gram-negative outer membrane

choices.label: D
choices.text: A single polar flagellum

questions.explanation: Endospores are dormant, extremely heat-resistant structures; boiling may not kill them, which is why autoclaving (steam under pressure) is used for sterilization.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: In cellular metabolism, what is substrate-level phosphorylation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the anaerobic regeneration of NAD+ from NADH so glycolysis can continue

choices.label: B
choices.text: the flow of protons through ATP synthase that drives ATP formation

choices.label: C
choices.text: the direct transfer of a phosphate group to ADP during a reaction

choices.label: D
choices.text: the cytoplasmic pathway that splits glucose into two pyruvate, netting 2 ATP and 2 NADH

questions.explanation: substrate-level phosphorylation is the direct transfer of a phosphate group to ADP during a reaction.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

choices.label: D

questions.stem: What does homologous recombination repair accomplish?

choices.label: A
choices.text: removal of a just-added incorrect nucleotide using 3' to 5' exonuclease activity

choices.label: B
choices.text: removal of bulky lesions such as UV-induced thymine dimers

choices.label: C
choices.text: accurate repair of a double-strand break using a sister chromatid as template

choices.label: D
choices.text: correction of base-pairing errors that escape polymerase proofreading

questions.explanation: homologous recombination repair: accurate repair of a double-strand break using a sister chromatid as template

questions.stem: In virology, what is a capsomere?

choices.label: D

questions.explanation: a capsomere is a protein subunit that assembles into the viral capsid.

questions.stem: In metabolic regulation, what is a catabolic pathway?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the key regulated enzyme controlling the committed step of glycolysis

choices.label: B

choices.label: C

choices.label: D

questions.stem: In cellular metabolism, what is fermentation?

choices.text: the anaerobic regeneration of NAD+ from NADH so glycolysis can continue

choices.label: B
choices.text: the matrix cycle that oxidizes acetyl-CoA to carbon dioxide, making NADH, FADH2, and GTP

choices.label: C
choices.text: the conversion of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA, releasing carbon dioxide and making NADH

choices.label: D
choices.text: the inner-membrane chain that passes electrons to build a proton gradient

questions.explanation: fermentation is the anaerobic regeneration of NAD+ from NADH so glycolysis can continue.

questions.stem: In evolution, what is a molecular clock?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a situation in which improving one trait comes at a cost to another

choices.label: B
choices.text: a species found naturally in only one geographic region

choices.label: C
choices.text: the use of mutation rate to estimate divergence times between lineages

choices.label: D
choices.text: a species that has changed little over long spans of geologic time

questions.explanation: a molecular clock is the use of mutation rate to estimate divergence times between lineages.

questions.stem: After a carbohydrate-rich meal, blood glucose rises and the pancreas releases a hormone that promotes glucose uptake and glycogen storage. Which hormone is this?

choices.text: Insulin

choices.label: C
choices.text: Adrenaline only

choices.label: D

questions.stem: In genetics, what is genetic linkage?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the random orientation of homologous pairs at metaphase I

choices.label: B
choices.text: a measure of genetic distance based on how often two loci are separated

choices.label: C
choices.text: the exchange of segments between homologous chromosomes during prophase I

choices.label: D
choices.text: the tendency of genes close together on a chromosome to be inherited together

questions.explanation: genetic linkage is the tendency of genes close together on a chromosome to be inherited together.

questions.stem: What is the primary mechanism of action of tetracycline?

choices.label: A
choices.text: inhibits the 30S ribosomal subunit to block protein synthesis

choices.label: B
choices.text: inhibits DNA gyrase (topoisomerase) to block DNA replication

choices.label: C
choices.text: inhibits the transpeptidase that cross-links peptidoglycan in the cell wall

choices.label: D
choices.text: inhibits bacterial RNA polymerase to block transcription

questions.explanation: tetracycline inhibits the 30S ribosomal subunit to block protein synthesis.
